title: Method Missing 👻
description: Catch calls to non-existent methods and create true metaprogramming magic.

slides.title: The Ghost Method 👻
slides.content:
Typically, if you call a method on an Object that doesn't exist (like `"hello".bark`), Ruby instantly crashes and burns with a `NoMethodError`.

But what if you could elegantly intercept that exact crash before it happens?

slides.title: The `method_missing` Hook
slides.content:
Deep inside every single Ruby object is a hidden, silent method called `method_missing`.

When Ruby fails to find the method you called, it desperately fires `method_missing` as its last dying breath before crashing. 

If you override this method in your class, you can do literal magic!

slides.title: Intercepting the Crash
slides.content: Run this! We call `robot.laser_blast`, which explicitly does not exist! But instead of crashing, our custom `method_missing` smoothly catches it and reacts!
slides.codeSnippet:
class Robot
  def method_missing(method_name, *arguments)
    puts "WARNING: I don't know how to '#{method_name}'!"
    puts "But I'll pretend I did!"
  end
end

r2d2 = Robot.new
# This should crash, but it doesn't!
r2d2.laser_blast

quizData.question: Which explicitly strictly named built-in Ruby method gracefully triggers precisely automatically right before an object crashes due to a `NoMethodError`?
quizData.explanation: `method_missing` is the ultimate magical safety net that enables dynamic method generation.

slides.title: Dynamic Magic ✨
slides.content:
This is called Metaprogramming (code that writes code). 

Major frameworks like Ruby on Rails heavily use `method_missing` to intelligently generate database query methods out of thin air dynamically based explicitly entirely on the exact words you type! (e.g. `User.find_by_email_and_name`)
